name: Anime Action
description: Anime-style action video prompts: cel-shaded 2D look, limited animation with strategic impact frames, speed lines, dramatic rim lighting, dynamic eye close-ups, and explosive auras. Covers shounen action, seinen drama, mecha, magical girl, slice-of-life, cyberpunk anime, isekai, and OP/ED montage style. Reference optional — required only when reproducing a named anime character, franchise, or specific real likeness.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — cel-shaded 2D stills of apex moments (eye close-up, mid-aura pulse, impact-frame freeze, transformation flash). Multi-ref accepted when user supplies a character sheet or arena reference. Prompt explicitly requests cel-shaded, flat-color shadows, defined edges — never photorealism.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter for default anime motion (limited animation reads well at 5s, 24fps). google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter when the shot needs 3+ choreography beats or a long parallax pan. 5s clips, generate_audio: false. Always cel-shaded — never realistic.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — optional. Used for narrator framing, internal monologue, or one-line battle cry. In-fight dialogue is rare; silence + impact sting is the dominant pattern. Language follows brief.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1. HormoziCaptions for shounen/OP energy (large, slammed in). MinimalCaptions for seinen/slice-of-life. Many cuts ship caption-free; on-screen onomatopoeia (BOOM, CRASH, WHOOSH) is preferred over caption text in action moments.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single cue per video. Profile by genre: orchestral brass + strings for shounen climax; J-rock electric guitar + driving drums for OP montage; minor-key piano/strings for emotional drama; synth + arpeggios for cyberpunk; acoustic guitar for slice-of-life. Pre-impact silence is mandatory — duck music to zero for 0.5-1.0s before the impact frame.

constants.structure: 2s hook (eye / aura / blade / petals) → main beat (limited animation, static pose with animated FX) → impact frame (white flash + cross highlight) → recovery / outro
constants.cameraDoctrine: static-frame-with-character-motion as default; whip-pan, snap-zoom, Dutch-angle, rotating shot reserved for dramatic beats
constants.soundDoctrine: silence-then-sting — quiet 0.5s before impact, then full orchestral/J-rock hit on the cross-frame
constants.animationDoctrine: two-key-frames-with-speed-lines (smear) over smooth interpolation; hold poses 0.5-1.0s between motion bursts

variationAxes: subgenre (shounen action, seinen drama, mecha, magical girl, isekai, slice-of-life, sports, horror, romance-comedy, cyberpunk)
energy register (peaceful slice-of-life ↔ explosive shounen climax)
color palette (warm golden-hour 75% sat ↔ saturated shounen 140% ↔ cyberpunk neon-on-black 150% ↔ desaturated seinen 60%)
hook type (eye close-up, speed-line burst, transformation flash, blade unsheath, aura explosion, sakura wind, title-card slam, silhouette-on-sky, impact frame, chibi pop)
camera (static, dramatic zoom, rotating, Dutch angle, parallax pan, POV slash, whip-pan, low-angle hero, overhead)
VO language (any — emotional arc reads visually; Japanese onomatopoeia overlay optional)
doNotCopyLiterally: Named anime characters, franchise mascots, signature moves tied to a specific IP, exact title-card typography from a known show. Vibe, technique, and visual language only.
referenceNotes: Optional. Required only when the user names a real anime character (Goku, Naruto, Sailor Moon, etc.), a specific franchise look, or wants a real person rendered in anime style. In those cases AGENTS.md hard rule #3 applies — refuse without uploaded reference. Generic 'shounen power-up scene' or 'magical girl transformation' needs no reference.
